findings.evidence: Relationship stakes are routed through administrative language at the key pressure points: snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:163, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:333, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:335, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:339, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:383.
findings.problem: Sanitization/timidity drift: when Peter names the relationship as a legal-moral threat, the prose largely defends with paperwork, witnesses, and kin labels instead of embodied intimate stakes. This defuses the chapter's hardest emotional pressure at the exact moment consequence should be most physically and psychologically present.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the confrontation and immediate aftermath to keep public speech strategic while restoring private embodiment: target snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:159, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:163, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:333, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:339, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:383, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:391. Add close-POV beats that specify what their bond costs in body and self-concept (breath, touch impulse, fear memory, protective reflex) rather than only legal positioning.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised span at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:333-340 includes (1) at least one concrete internal sensation or memory tied to Elsie/Noreen intimacy and (2) one immediate physical reaction that carries into snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:383-391; FAIL if the defense remains only procedural/legal language.

findings.evidence: The Saint Brigid hearing runs 13 consecutive paragraph turns with no focalizer interior cue from Noreen: snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:197 through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:221.
findings.problem: Interiority drop-out: the chapter temporarily becomes transcript-like, weakening emotional tracking and violating close-third texture expectations.
findings.rewrite_direction: Insert interior beats in the hearing exchange, especially after leverage shifts around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:205, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:217, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:221. Keep them brief but specific (sensation, memory flash, self-assessment) so dialogue pressure remains intact.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:197-221 contains at least 2 explicit Noreen interior cues separated by dialogue turns and no run longer than 6 consecutive paragraphs without focalizer interiority; FAIL otherwise.

findings.evidence: The same comparative scaffold repeats 13 times ("as if") across the chapter, including snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:3, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:35, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:125, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:185, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:337, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:365.
findings.problem: Recurring prose formula: repeated "as if" construction flattens rhythm and signals autopilot rather than deliberate variation.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework repeated comparison lines in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:33, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:35, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:125, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:183, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:189, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:337, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:365 by replacing formulaic similes with direct observation, action consequence, or voice-specific metaphor.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if at least 4 of the 6 cited instances are rewritten without "as if" and each replacement adds concrete sensory or behavioral specificity in the same sentence; FAIL if the cited lines keep the same comparative template.

findings.evidence: In the legal-threat exchange, Noreen and Elsie converge toward similarly formal legal cadence: snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:327, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:335, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:339, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:345.
findings.problem: Idiolect separation weakens under pressure. Peter's formality is character-true, but Noreen and Elsie both shift into comparable declarative/legal diction, reducing voice distinctness.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the confrontation block at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:327, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:335, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:339, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:345 so Peter remains formal/administrative, Noreen regains rhythmic pivots and socially deft edge, and Elsie speaks in concrete domestic-risk pragmatics rather than parallel legal rhetoric.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:327-345 each speaker can be identified without dialogue tags by register alone (Peter formal/legal, Noreen socially agile and high-contraction, Elsie concrete and practical); FAIL if Elsie and Noreen lines remain interchangeable in syntax/rhythm.

findings.evidence: Three major sequences run the same demand-counter-terms pattern: debt collector schedule at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:59-95, nursery-pass hearing at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:191-249, and Peter/Tom doorstep terms at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:319-351.
findings.problem: Structural repetition mutes escalation. The chapter keeps re-solving pressure through administrative bargaining beats, so later confrontations feel like variants of prior paperwork scenes instead of fresh narrative movement.
findings.rewrite_direction: Differentiate one of the three negotiation engines into an action-led beat with an irreversible consequence, focusing edits on snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:319, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:327, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:343, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:351 (or alternatively compress snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:59 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:93). Preserve terms-setting, but change scene mechanics so conflict is not resolved by another near-identical verbal contract.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if at least one targeted sequence ends with a new on-page material consequence (not just another stated condition), and the three cited blocks no longer share the same demand-counter-written-terms resolution shape; FAIL if all three still resolve primarily as procedural negotiations.

findings.evidence: Peter announces a six o'clock call at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:251, then arrives at the exact peak of Tom's confrontation at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:317 without additional causal bridge.
findings.problem: Scene orchestration feels stage-managed rather than inevitable; coincidence pressure is visible to the reader and weakens causal realism.
findings.rewrite_direction: Add a causal bridge in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:253, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:255, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:291, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:305, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:317 (for example: someone observes Peter approaching, Tom delays intentionally to coincide, or Peter clocks Tom entering and follows) so overlap reads as consequence, not convenience.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:253-317 contains a specific trigger that explains the overlap and is acknowledged in dialogue or narration at arrival; FAIL if Peter's entrance remains an unprepared punctual pileup.

summary: Continuity and tense checks are stable (no canonical contradiction found against outline/continuity_sheet.json, and narration remains in past tense), but the chapter fails review due a HIGH sanitization/timidity issue at the relationship-pressure apex plus unresolved medium issues in structural escalation, dialogue idiolect separation, interiority continuity, and repetitive prose constructions.
